      apply HasDerivAt.sub
      · -- The function `fun s => lam` has derivative 0 at t
        apply hasDerivAt_const
      · -- The function `fun s => s` has derivative 1 at t
        exact hasDerivAt_id t
    all_goals
      have h1 : lam - t ≠ 0 := by linarith
      field_simp [h1]
      all_goals try { linarith }
  · -- Prove that 1/(lam - t)^2 ≤ f t (α t)
    have h1 : lam - t ≠ 0 := by linarith
    have h2 : 0 ≤ Real.sin (t * (α t)) ^ 2 := by positivity
    have h3 : f t (α t) = (α t) ^ 2 + Real.sin (t * (α t)) ^ 2 := by
      simp [f]
    have h4 : (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2 : ℝ) = (α t) ^ 2 := by
      have h5 : α t = 1 / (lam - t) := by
        simp [α]
      rw [h5]
      field_simp [(show lam - t ≠ 0 by linarith)]
    rw [h4]
    have h7 : (α t) ^ 2 ≤ f t (α t) := by
      linarith [h3, h2]
    linarith

theorem subq_II_3_a
    (lam : ℝ) (t : ℝ) (ht : t < lam) :
    let α : ℝ → ℝ := fun s => 1 / (lam - s)
    let f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ := fun s x => x ^ 2 + Real.sin (s * x) ^ 2
    HasDerivAt α (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) t ∧
      (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) ≤ f t (α t) := by
  intro α f
  constructor
  · -- Show that α has derivative 1/(c-t)^2 at t
    have hc : lam - t ≠ 0 := by linarith
    have h2 : HasDerivAt α (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) t := by
      have h3 : α = fun s : ℝ => 1 / (lam - s) := by rfl
      rw [h3]
      apply HasDerivAt.div
      · -- The function `fun s => 1` has derivative 0 at t
        apply hasDerivAt_const
      · -- The function `fun s => c - s` has derivative -1 at t
        simp [hasDerivAt_id', sub_eq_add_neg]
      all_goals
        try { field_simp [show lam - t ≠ 0 by linarith] }
    exact h2
  · -- Show that 1/(c-t)^2 <= f t (α t)
    have h4 : f t (α t) = (α t) ^ 2 + Real.sin (t * (α t)) ^ 2 := by
      simp [f]
    rw [h4]
    have h5 : α t = 1 / (lam - t) := by
      rfl
    rw [h5]
    have h6 : 0 ≤ Real.sin (t * (1 / (lam - t))) ^ 2 := by
      positivity
    have h7 : (1 / (lam - t) : ℝ) ^ 2 ≤ (1 / (lam - t) : ℝ) ^ 2 + Real.sin (t * (1 / (lam - t))) ^ 2 := by
      linarith [h6]
    have h8 : (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2 : ℝ) = (1 / (lam - t) : ℝ) ^ 2 := by
      ring_nf
    rw [h8]
    linarith [h7]

theorem subq_II_3_a
    (lam : ℝ) (t : ℝ) (ht : t < lam) :
    let α : ℝ → ℝ := fun s => 1 / (lam - s)
    let f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ := fun s x => x ^ 2 + Real.sin (s * x) ^ 2
    HasDerivAt α (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) t ∧
      (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) ≤ f t (α t) := by
  intro α f
  constructor
  · -- Show that α has the specified derivative at t
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => (1 : ℝ)) (0 : ℝ) t := by
      apply hasDerivAt_const
    have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => (lam - s : ℝ)) (-1 : ℝ) t := by
      apply HasDerivAt.sub
      · apply hasDerivAt_const
      · apply hasDerivAt_id
    have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => 1 / (lam - s : ℝ)) (1 / (lam - t : ℝ) ^ 2) t := by
      apply HasDerivAt.div h1 h2
      all_goals
        have h4 : (lam - t : ℝ) ≠ 0 := by linarith
        all_goals try { field_simp [h4] }
        all_goals try { linarith }
    simpa using h3
  · -- Show the inequality
    have ht2 : (lam - t : ℝ) > 0 := by linarith
    have h5 : f t (α t) = (α t) ^ 2 + Real.sin (t * (α t)) ^ 2 := by
      simp [f]
    have h6 : α t = 1 / (lam - t : ℝ) := by
      simp [α]
    rw [h5, h6]
    have h7 : (1 / (lam - t : ℝ) : ℝ) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h8 : Real.sin (t * (1 / (lam - t : ℝ)) ) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h9 : (1 / (lam - t : ℝ) : ℝ) ^ 2 + Real.sin (t * (1 / (lam - t : ℝ)) ) ^ 2 ≥ (1 / (lam - t : ℝ) ) ^ 2 := by linarith
    have h10 : (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2 : ℝ) = (1 / (lam - t : ℝ) : ℝ) ^ 2 := by
      ring_nf
    nlinarith

theorem subq_II_3_a
    (lam : ℝ) (t : ℝ) (ht : t < lam) :
    let α : ℝ → ℝ := fun s => 1 / (lam - s)
    let f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ := fun s x => x ^ 2 + Real.sin (s * x) ^ 2
    HasDerivAt α (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) t ∧
      (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2) ≤ f t (α t) := by
  let α : ℝ → ℝ := fun s => 1 / (lam - s)
  let f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ := fun s x => x ^ 2 + Real.sin (s * x) ^ 2
  constructor
  · -- First part, proving the derivative
    apply HasDerivAt.div
    · -- Show that `fun s => 1` has a derivative at `t` equal to 0
      apply hasDerivAt_const
    · -- Show that `fun s => lam - s` has a derivative at `t` equal to -1
      apply HasDerivAt.sub
      · -- First part of the subtraction
        apply hasDerivAt_const
      · -- Second part of the subtraction
        exact hasDerivAt_id t
    · -- Show that the denominator is non-zero at t
      have h1 : lam - t ≠ 0 := by linarith
      exact h1
  · -- Show that 1/(x)^2 <= f t (α t)
    have h1 : (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2 : ℝ) ≤ f t (α t) := by
      dsimp [f, α]
      have h3 : 0 ≤ Real.sin (t * (1 / (lam - t))) ^ 2 := by positivity
      have h4 : (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2 : ℝ) ≤ (1 / (lam - t) ^ 2 : ℝ) + Real.sin (t * (1 / (lam - t))) ^ 2 := by linarith
      linarith
    linarith

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
    have h2 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
      specialize hu (τ + T)
      simpa using hu
    have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 τ := by
      apply hasDerivAt_id
    have h5 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) * 1) τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      exact h2
      exact h4
    simp at h5 ⊢
    exact h5
  have h2 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    ring_nf at f_periodic ⊢
    linarith
  rw [h2] at h1
  exact h1

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)

    have h6 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 τ := by
      apply hasDerivAt_id
    have h7 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (1 * (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)))) τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      · exact h2
      · exact h6
    have h8 : (1 : ℝ) * (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) = f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) := by ring
    rw [h8] at h7
    exact h7
  have h9 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic
  rw [h9] at h1
  exact h1

      · -- Show that $u$ has a derivative at $\tau + T$.

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
    have h4 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
      specialize hu (τ + T)
      simpa using hu
    have h5 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.add
      · exact hasDerivAt_id τ
      · apply hasDerivAt_const
    have h6 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (1 * (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) )) τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      · exact h4
      · exact h5
    have h7 : (1 * (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) ) : ℝ) = f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) := by ring
    rw [h7] at h6
    exact h6
  have h8 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic
  rw [h8] at h1
  exact h1

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h1 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
    specialize hu (τ + T)
    simpa using hu
  have h2 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
    have h23 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 τ := by
      simp [hasDerivAt_id']
    have h24 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      · exact h23
      · exact h1
    exact h24
  have h3 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic
  rw [h3] at h2
  exact h2

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
    have h2 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
      specialize hu (τ + T)
      simpa using hu
    have h5 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      · -- Show that $g(s) = s + T$ has a derivative at $\tau$.
        apply hasDerivAt_id
      · -- Show that $u$ has a derivative at $g(\tau) = \tau + T$.
        exact h2
    exact h5
  have h2 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic
  rw [h2] at h1
  exact h1

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h4 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
    specialize hu (τ + T)
    simpa using hu
  have h18 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 (τ) := by
    simp [hasDerivAt_id']
  have h19 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ) := by
    apply HasDerivAt.comp
    · exact h4
    all_goals
      try { exact h18 }
  have h10 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic (τ) (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic
  rw [h10] at h19
  exact h19

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :

      specialize hu (τ + T)
      simpa using hu
    have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 τ := by
      simp [hasDerivAt_id']
    have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) * 1) τ := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      exact h2
      exact h3
    simpa using h4
  have h2 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h1 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
    specialize hu (τ + T)
    simpa using hu
  have h2 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    ring_nf at f_periodic ⊢
    linarith
  have h3 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
    have h57 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => s + T) 1 τ := by
      apply hasDerivAt_id'
    have h58 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ) := by
      apply HasDerivAt.comp
      · -- Show that u has a derivative at (τ + T)
        exact h1
      · -- Show that s ↦ s + T has a derivative 1 at s = τ
        exact h57
    exact h58
  rw [show f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) by linarith [h2]] at h3
  exact h3

theorem subq_IV_2_a
    (T : ℝ) (f : ℝ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_periodic : ∀ t x : ℝ, f (t + T) x = f t x)
    (u : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hu : ∀ t : ℝ, HasDerivAt u (f t (u t)) t) :
    ∀ τ : ℝ,
      HasDerivAt (fun s => u (s + T))
        (f τ (u (τ + T))) τ := by
  intro τ
  have h3 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := by
    apply hu
  have h4 : HasDerivAt (fun s : ℝ => u (s + T)) (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) τ := by
    apply HasDerivAt.comp
    · exact h3
    · -- Show that the function s ↦ s + T has a derivative at τ equal to 1
      simp [hasDerivAt_id']
  have h8 : f (τ + T) (u (τ + T)) = f τ (u (τ + T)) := by
    specialize f_periodic τ (u (τ + T))
    simpa using f_periodic
  rw [h8] at h4
  exact h4

    have h9 : HasDerivAt u (f (τ + T) (u (τ + T))) (τ + T) := h1

theorem subq_I_3_b
    (q : ℝ) (hq : 0 < q)
    (u₁ u₂ : ℝ → ℝ)
    (p : ℝ)
    (hp : ∀ t : ℝ,
      (u₁ t) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 + (u₂ t) ^ 2 = p) :
    0 ≤ p ∧ (p = 0 → ∀ t : ℝ, u₁ t = 0 ∧ u₂ t = 0) := by 
  have h1 : 0 ≤ p := by
    have h2 := hp 0
    have h3 : (u₁ 0) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ 0) ^ 4 + (u₂ 0) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by
      positivity
    linarith [h2, h3]

  have h2 : p = 0 → ∀ t : ℝ, u₁ t = 0 ∧ u₂ t = 0 := by
    intro hp0
    intro t
    have h_eq := hp t
    rw [hp0] at h_eq
    have h3 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 + (u₂ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by linarith
    have h4 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      nlinarith [sq_nonneg (u₁ t), sq_nonneg (u₂ t), sq_nonneg ((u₁ t) ^ 2), mul_nonneg (show 0 ≤ q / 2 by nlinarith) (show 0 ≤ (u₁ t) ^ 4 by positivity)]
    have h5 : u₁ t = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h6 : (u₂ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      nlinarith [sq_nonneg (u₁ t), sq_nonneg (u₂ t), sq_nonneg ((u₁ t) ^ 2), mul_nonneg (show 0 ≤ q / 2 by nlinarith) (show 0 ≤ (u₁ t) ^ 4 by positivity)]
    have h7 : u₂ t = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    exact ⟨h5, h7⟩

  constructor
  · exact h1
  · exact h2

theorem subq_I_3_b
    (q : ℝ) (hq : 0 < q)
    (u₁ u₂ : ℝ → ℝ)
    (p : ℝ)
    (hp : ∀ t : ℝ,
      (u₁ t) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 + (u₂ t) ^ 2 = p) :
    0 ≤ p ∧ (p = 0 → ∀ t : ℝ, u₁ t = 0 ∧ u₂ t = 0) := by
  have h1 : 0 ≤ p := by
    have h2 := hp 0
    nlinarith [sq_nonneg (u₁ 0), sq_nonneg (u₂ 0), sq_nonneg ((u₁ 0) ^ 2), mul_nonneg (show 0 ≤ q by linarith) (show 0 ≤ (u₁ 0) ^ 4 by positivity), sq_nonneg (q / 2), mul_nonneg (show 0 ≤ q / 2 by nlinarith) (show 0 ≤ (u₁ 0) ^ 4 by positivity)]
  constructor
  · exact h1
  · intro hp0 t
    have h_eq : (u₁ t) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 + (u₂ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      specialize hp t
      rw [hp0] at hp
      linarith
    have h1 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h2 : (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 ≥ 0 := by
      have hq2 : 0 < q / 2 := by nlinarith
      positivity
    have h3 : (u₂ t) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h4 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h5 : u₁ t = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h6 : (u₂ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h7 : u₂ t = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    exact ⟨h5, h7⟩

theorem subq_I_3_b
    (q : ℝ) (hq : 0 < q)
    (u₁ u₂ : ℝ → ℝ)
    (p : ℝ)
    (hp : ∀ t : ℝ,
      (u₁ t) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 + (u₂ t) ^ 2 = p) :
    0 ≤ p ∧ (p = 0 → ∀ t : ℝ, u₁ t = 0 ∧ u₂ t = 0) := by
  have h1 : 0 ≤ p := by
    have eq1 := hp 0
    have h2 : (u₁ 0) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ 0) ^ 4 + (u₂ 0) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by
      positivity
    linarith [eq1, h2]

  have h2 : p = 0 → ∀ t : ℝ, u₁ t = 0 ∧ u₂ t = 0 := by
    intro hp0
    intro t
    have eq2 := hp t
    rw [hp0] at eq2
    have h3 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 + (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 + (u₂ t) ^ 2 = (0 : ℝ) := by linarith
    have h4 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h5 : (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 ≥ 0 := by
      positivity
    have h6 : (u₂ t) ^ 2 ≥ 0 := by positivity
    have h7 : (u₁ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h8 : (u₂ t) ^ 2 = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h9 : u₁ t = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    have h10 : u₂ t = 0 := by
      nlinarith
    exact ⟨h9, h10⟩

  constructor
  · exact h1
  · exact h2

theorem subq_I_3_b

    have h5 : (q / 2) * (u₁ t) ^ 4 ≥ 0 := by positivity

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  by_cases h : n = 1
  · -- Case n = 1
    rw [h]
    use 1
    constructor
    · -- Show that 1 is a solution
      constructor
      · norm_num
      constructor
      · norm_num
      · -- Show that f 1 1 = 0
        rw [f_def 1 1]
        norm_num
    · -- Show that 1 is the unique solution
      intro u hu
      rcases hu with ⟨hu0, hu1, h_eq⟩
      rw [f_def 1 u] at h_eq
      have h_eq2 : u = 1 := by
        norm_num [Finset.sum_range_succ] at h_eq
        all_goals linarith
      linarith
  · -- Case n > 1
    have h2 : n > 1 := by
      by_contra h'
      push_neg at h'
      have : n = 1 := by omega
      tauto
    have h3 : n ≥ 2 := by omega
    have eq1 : f n 0 = -1 := by
      rw [f_def n 0]
      have h4 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
        have h5 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
          intro k
          simp
        apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
        intro k hk
        specialize h5 k
        simpa using h5
      linarith
    have eq2 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
      rw [f_def n 1]
      have h7 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
        have h8 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
          intro k
          simp
        have h9 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) := by
          apply Finset.sum_congr
          · rfl
          · intro k hk
            specialize h8 k
            simpa using h8
        rw [h9]
        simp
      linarith
    have h12 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
      have h13 : f n = fun x : ℝ => (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
        funext x
        exact f_def n x
      rw [h13]
      apply Continuous.sub
      · apply continuous_finset_sum
        intro k hk
        apply continuous_pow
      · apply continuous_const
    have h18 : ∃ c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ), f n c = 0 := by
      have h19 : (f n (0 : ℝ)) * (f n (1 : ℝ)) ≤ 0 := by
        rw [eq1, eq2]
        have hn2 : (n : ℝ) ≥ (2 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast (show n ≥ 2 by omega)
        have hn3 : (n : ℝ) - 1 ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by linarith
        nlinarith
      have h20 : ∃ c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ), f n c = 0 := by
        apply Continuous.exists_zero_of_mul_nonpos _ h12
        · -- Show that $0 \in \text{Set.Icc } 0 1$
          exact ⟨by norm_num, by norm_num⟩
        · -- Show that $1 \in \text{Set.Icc } 0 1$
          exact ⟨by norm_num, by norm_num⟩
        all_goals nlinarith
      exact h20
    rcases h18 with ⟨c, hc, hc1⟩
    have hc2 : 0 ≤ c := by
      have h19 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hc
      exact h19.1
    have hc3 : c ≤ 1 := by
      have h19 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hc
      exact h19.2
    use c
    constructor
    · -- Show that c is a solution
      constructor
      · linarith
      constructor
      · linarith
      · -- Show that f n c = 0
        exact hc1
    · -- Show that c is the unique solution
      intro u hu
      rcases hu with ⟨hu0, hu1, h_eq⟩
      have h_eq1 : f n u = 0 := h_eq
      have h_eq2 : f n c = 0 := hc1
      have h10 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
      have h11 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
      have h12 : c ≥ 0 := by linarith
      have h13 : c ≤ 1 := by linarith
      by_cases h14 : u < c
      · -- Assume u < c
        have h15 : u < c := by linarith
        have h18 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) = 1 := by
          have h19 : f n u = 0 := h_eq1
          rw [f_def n u] at h19
          linarith
        have h19 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) = 1 := by
          have h20 : f n c = 0 := h_eq2
          rw [f_def n c] at h20
          linarith
        have h20 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) := by
          have h21 : u < c := h15
          have h22 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h23 : c ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h24 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h25 : c ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h26 : ∀ k : ℕ, u ^ (k + 1) ≤ c ^ (k + 1) := by
            intro k
            have h27 : u ≤ c := by linarith
            have h28 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ c ^ (k + 1) := by
              apply pow_le_pow_left
              all_goals nlinarith
            linarith
          have h27 : (∃ k ∈ Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1) < c ^ (k + 1)) := by
            use 0
            simp
            all_goals nlinarith
          have h28 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) := by
            apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
            · -- Show that for all k in range n, u^(k+1) ≤ c^(k+1)
              intro k hk
              specialize h26 k
              linarith
            · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that u^(k+1) < c^(k+1)
              exact h27
          linarith
        nlinarith
      · -- Case c ≤ u
        by_cases h15 : c < u
        · -- Assume c < u
          have h15 : c < u := by linarith
          have h18 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) = 1 := by
            have h19 : f n u = 0 := h_eq1
            rw [f_def n u] at h19
            linarith
          have h19 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) = 1 := by
            have h20 : f n c = 0 := h_eq2
            rw [f_def 11 c] at h20
            linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h4 : f n 0 = -1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h21 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
      have h12 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        intro k
        have h13 : k + 1 > 0 := by omega
        simp [h13]
      apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
      intro k hk
      specialize h12 k
      simpa using h12
    rw [h21]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h5 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h22 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h12 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
        intro k
        induction k with
        | zero =>
          norm_num
        | succ k ih =>
          simp [pow_succ] at ih ⊢
          all_goals linarith
      rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => h12 k)]
      simp
    rw [h22]
    all_goals ring
  have h50 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [h5]
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast (show n ≥ 1 by linarith)
    nlinarith
  have h6 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
    rw [f_def]
    apply Continuous.sub
    · apply continuous_finsetSum
      intro k hk
      apply continuous_pow
    · continuity
  have h7 : ∃ u, u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
    have h8 : f n 0 < (0 : ℝ) := by linarith [h4]
    have h9 : (0 : ℝ) ≤ f n 1 := by linarith [h50]
    apply Continuous.exists_mem_set_of_le_of_lt_of_lt
    · exact h6
    · norm_num
    · norm_num
    all_goals try linarith
  rcases h7 with ⟨u, hu, h_eq⟩
  have h10 : 0 ≤ u := by
    have h11 : u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hu
    exact h11.1
  have h11 : u ≤ 1 := by
    have h11 : u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hu
    exact h11.2
  use u
  constructor
  · -- Show that u is a solution
    refine ⟨by linarith, by linarith, h_eq⟩
  · -- Show that u is unique
    intro v h
    rcases h with ⟨hv0, hv1, h_eq2⟩
    by_cases h16 : v < u
    · -- Assume v < u
      have h17 : f n v < f n u := by
        have h14 : f n v = (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          rw [f_def]
        have h15 : f n u = (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          rw [f_def]
        rw [h14, h15]
        have h16 : v < u := by linarith
        have h17 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h18 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h19 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h20 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h21 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) := by
          apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
          · -- Show that for all k, v^(k+1) ≤ u^(k+1)
            intro k hk
            have h22 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
              have h23 : v ≤ u := by linarith
              have h24 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h25 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h26 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
                have h11 : k ≥ 0 := by omega
                omega
              have h27 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              linarith
            linarith
          · -- Show that there exists at least one k such that v^(k+1) < u^(k+1)
            use 0
            have h28 : v ^ (0 + 1) < u ^ (0 + 1) := by
              have h29 : v < u := by linarith
              have h30 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h31 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h32 : 0 + 1 ≥ 1 := by norm_num
              have h33 : v ^ (0 + 1) < u ^ (0 + 1) := by
                apply pow_lt_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              linarith
            linarith
        have h22 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
        linarith
      linarith [h_eq, h_eq2, h17]
    by_cases h17 : v > u
    · -- Assume v > u
      have h18 : f n u < f n v := by
        have h14 : f n u = (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          rw [f_def]
        have h15 : f n v = (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          rw [f_def]
        rw [h14, h15]
        have h16 : u < v := by linarith
        have h17 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h18 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h19 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h20 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h21 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) := by
          apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
          · -- Show  that  for  all  k,  u^(k+1) ≤ v^(k+1)
            intro k hk
            have h22 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ v ^ (k + 1) := by
              have h23 : u ≤ v := by linarith
              have h24 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h25 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h26 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
                have h11 : k ≥ 0 := by omega
                omega
              have h27 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ v ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              linarith
            linarith
          · -- Show  that  there  exists  at  least  one  k  such  that  u^(k+1)  <  v^(k+1)
            use 0
            have h28 : u ^ (0 + 1) < v ^ (0 + 1) := by
              have h29 : u < v := by linarith
              have h30 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h31 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h32 : 0 + 1 ≥ 1 := by norm_num
              have h33 : u ^ (0 + 1) < v ^ (0 + 1) := by
                apply pow_lt_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              linarith
            linarith
        have h22 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
        linarith
      linarith [h_eq, h_eq2, h18]
    · -- v = u
      have h18 : v ≤ u := by linarith
      have h19 : v ≥ u := by linarith
      have h20 : v = u := by linarith
      tauto

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  set f_n : ℝ → ℝ := fun x => f n x
  have hf_n : ∀ x : ℝ, f_n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
    intro x
    specialize f_def n x
    simpa using f_def
  have h2 : f_n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    have h3 : f_n 1 = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := hf_n 1
    rw [h3]
    have h4 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h5 : ∀ (k : ℕ), (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
        intro k
        simp
      rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => h5 k)]
      simp
    rw [h4]
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast (show n ≥ 1 by linarith)
    nlinarith
  have h3 : f_n 0 = -1 := by
    have h3 : f_n 0 = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := hf_n 0
    rw [h3]
    have h4 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
      have h7 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ∈ Finset.range n → (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        intro k hk
        have h8 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by omega
        simp [zero_pow, h8]
      apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
      intro k hk
      specialize h7 k hk
      all_goals try linarith
    rw [h4]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h8 : Continuous f_n := by
    have h9 : f_n = fun x : ℝ => (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      funext x
      exact hf_n x
    rw [h9]
    continuity
  have h9 : ∃ c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, f_n c = 0 := by
    apply IVT_of_continuous_of_le_of_lt
    · exact h8
    · -- Show that 0 ≤ (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1
      norm_num
    · -- Show that 0 ≤ (1 : ℝ) ≤ 1
      norm_num
    · -- Show that f_n 0 ≤ 0
      have h12 : f_n 0 = -1 := h3
      linarith
    · -- Show that 0 ≤ f_n 1
      linarith [h2]
  rcases h9 with ⟨c, hc, hc0⟩
  have hc1 : 0 ≤ c := by
    have h1 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hc
    exact h1.1
  have hc2 : c ≤ 1 := by
    have h1 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hc
    exact h1.2
  have h10 : StrictMonoOn f_n (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
    intro x hx y hy hxy
    rw [hf_n x, hf_n y]
    have hx0 : 0 ≤ x := by
      have h1 : x ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hx
      exact h1.1
    have hx1 : x ≤ 1 := by
      have h1 : x ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hx
      exact h1.2
    have hy0 : 0 ≤ y := by
      have h1 : y ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hy
      exact h1.1
    have hy1 : y ≤ 1 := by
      have h1 : y ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hy
      exact h1.2
    have h12 : x < y := by linarith
    have h14 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      have h14 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) := by
        have h15 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ∈ Finset.range n → x ^ (k + 1) < y ^ (k + 1) := by
          intro k hk
          have h16 : x < y := h12
          have h17 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by omega
          have h18 : x ^ (k + 1) < y ^ (k + 1) := by
            apply pow_lt_pow_left
            all_goals nlinarith
          assumption
        apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
        · -- Show that for all k in range n, x^(k+1) ≤ y^(k+1)
          intro k hk
          have h16 : x ≤ y := by linarith
          have h17 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by omega
          have h18 : x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1) := by
            apply pow_le_pow_left
            all_goals nlinarith
          assumption
        · -- Show that there exists at least one k in range n such that x^(k+1) < y^(k+1)
          use 0
          constructor
          · -- Show that 0 ∈ range n
            simp
            omega
          · -- Show that x^1 < y^1
            have h16 : x < y := h12
            have h17 : x ^ 1 < y ^ 1 := by nlinarith
            assumption
      linarith
    assumption
  refine' ⟨c, ⟨hc1, hc2, hc0⟩, _⟩
  intro d ⟨hd0, hd1, h15⟩
  have h16 : d ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
    exact ⟨by linarith, by linarith⟩
  have h17 : f_n d = 0 := by linarith
  have h18 : f_n c = 0 := hc0
  by_cases h19 : d < c
  · -- Assume d < c
    have h20 : f_n d < f_n c := by
      apply h10
      · -- Show d ∈ [0,1]
        exact h16
      · -- Show c ∈ [0,1]
        have h17 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := ⟨by linarith, by linarith⟩
        assumption
      all_goals try linarith
    have h21 : (0 : ℝ) < (0 : ℝ) := by linarith [h17, h20, h18]
    all_goals try linarith
  · -- Assume ¬(d < c)
    by_cases h20 : d > c
    · -- Assume d > c
      have h21 : f_n d > f_n c := by
        apply h10
        · -- Show c ∈ [0,1]
          have h17 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := ⟨by linarith, by linarith⟩
          assumption
        · -- Show d ∈ [0,1]
          exact h16
        all_goals try linarith
      have h22 : (0 : ℝ) > (0 : ℝ) := by linarith [h17, h21, h18]
      all_goals try linarith
    · -- Assume d ≤ c and not d > c
      have h23 : d = c := by linarith
      assumption

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h2 : f n 0 = -1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    simp
  have h3 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h5 : ∀ (n : ℕ), ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
        intro n
        induction n with
        | zero =>
          simp
        | succ n ih =>
          rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, ih]
          all_goals norm_num
      apply h5 n
    simp [h4]
  have h4 : ∃ u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
    by_cases h : n > 1
    · -- If n > 1, then f n 1 ≥ 1
      have h6 : f n 1 ≥ 1 := by
        have hn1 : (n : ℝ) ≥ (2 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast show n ≥ 2 by omega
        have h7 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := h3
        linarith
      have h14 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
        funext x
        rw [f_def]
        apply continuous_finset_sum
        intro k hk
        continuity
      have h15 : ∃ c : ℝ, c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) ∧ f n c = (0 : ℝ) := by
        apply h14.existsIntermediateValue (by norm_num) (by norm_num) (by
          have h11 : f n 0 < (0 : ℝ) := by linarith [h2]
          have h12 : (0 : ℝ) < f n 1 := by linarith [h6]
          all_goals linarith
        )
      rcases h15 with ⟨c, hc1, hc2⟩
      refine ⟨c, ?_⟩
      constructor
      · -- Show 0 ≤ c
        have h16 : c ≥ 0 := by
          have h17 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hc1
          exact h17.1
        linarith
      constructor
      · -- Show c ≤ 1
        have h17 : c ≤ 1 := by
          have h18 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hc1
          exact h18.2
        linarith
      · -- Show f n c = 0
        linarith [hc2]
    · -- If n = 1
      have h7 : n = 1 := by omega
      rw [h7] at *
      refine ⟨1, by norm_num, by norm_num, ?_⟩
      norm_num [f_def]
  have h18 : ∀ x : ℝ, x ≥ 0 → f n x = (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
    intro x hx
    specialize f_def n x
    simpa using f_def
  have h19 : StrictMonoOn (fun x : ℝ => f n x) (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ)) := by
    intro x hx y hy hxy
    have hx0 : 0 ≤ x := by
      have h1 : x ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hx
      exact h1.1
    have hx1 : x ≤ 1 := by
      have h1 : x ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hx
      exact h1.2
    have hy0 : 0 ≤ y := by
      have h1 : y ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hy
      exact h1.1
    have hy1 : y ≤ 1 := by
      have h1 : y ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hy
      exact h1.2
    have h20 : x < y := by linarith
    have h21 : f n x < f n y := by
      have h18x : f n x = (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := h18 x (by linarith)
      have h18y : f n y = (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := h18 y (by linarith)
      rw [h18x, h18y]
      have h22 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (y : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) > ∑ k in Finset.range n, (x : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
        apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
        · -- Show that for all k in range n, (y : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≥ (x : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)
          intro k hk
          have h23 : (x : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (y : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
            have h24 : x ≤ y := by linarith
            have h25 : (x : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (y : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
              apply pow_le_pow_left
              all_goals nlinarith
            linarith
          linarith
        · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that (y : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) > (x : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)
          use 0
          constructor
          · -- Show 0 ∈ range n
            simp at *
            all_goals omega
          · -- Show (y : ℝ) ^ (0 + 1) > (x : ℝ) ^ (0 + 1)
            have h26 : x < y := by linarith
            have h27 : (x : ℝ) ^ (1 : ℕ) < (y : ℝ) ^ (1 : ℕ) := by
              nlinarith
            simpa using h27
      have h23 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (y : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 > (∑ k in Finset.range n, (x : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
        linarith
      linarith
    exact h21
  rcases h4 with ⟨c, hc0, hc1, h_eq⟩
  use c
  constructor
  · -- Show that c is indeed a solution
    tauto
  · -- Show that it's unique
    intro u hu
    rcases hu with ⟨hu0, hu1, h_eq2⟩
    have h23 : f n u = 0 := h_eq2
    have h24 : f n c = 0 := h_eq
    by_cases h : u < c
    · -- Case u < c
      have h25 : f n u < f n c := by
        apply h19
        · -- Show u ∈ Icc 0 1
          constructor
          · linarith
          · linarith
        · -- Show c ∈ Icc 0 1
          constructor
          · linarith
          · linarith
        all_goals linarith
      have h26 : f n u < f n c := h25
      linarith
    by_cases h' : c < u
    · -- Case c < u
      have h29 : f n c < f n u := by
        apply h19
        · -- Show c ∈ Icc 0 1
          constructor
          · linarith
          · linarith
        · -- Show u ∈ Icc 0 1
          constructor
          · linarith
          · linarith
        all_goals linarith
      linarith
    · -- Case u = c
      have h32 : u ≥ c := by linarith
      have h33 : u ≤ c := by linarith
      have h34 : u = c := by linarith
      linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h2 : f n 0 = -1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h3 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
      have h4 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ≥ 1 → (0 : ℝ) ^ k = 0 := by
        intro k hk
        have h5 : k ≥ 1 := by omega
        have : (0 : ℝ) ^ k = 0 := by
          apply zero_pow
          omega
        assumption
      have h6 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ∈ Finset.range n → (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        intro k hk
        have h7 : k < n := by
          simp at hk ⊢
          all_goals omega
        have h8 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
          omega
        have h9 : (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
          apply h4 (k + 1) h8
        assumption
      have h10 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
        apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
        intro k hk
        specialize h6 k hk
        simpa using h6
      assumption
    rw [h3]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h3 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h4 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h5 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
        intro k
        induction k with
        | zero => norm_num
        | succ k ih =>
          simp [pow_succ] at ih ⊢
      have h6 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
        rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => h5 k)]
        simp
      assumption
    rw [h4]
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast show n ≥ 1 by linarith
    nlinarith
  have h4 : ∃ u, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
    by_cases h : n > 1
    · -- If n > 1, then f n 0 = -1 < 0
      have h8 : f n 0 < 0 := by
        rw [h2]
        all_goals norm_num
      have h9 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := h3
      have h10 : ∃ c, 0 < c ∧ c ≤ 1 ∧ f n c = 0 := by
        apply exists_between_of_lt_of_le (by linarith) (by linarith)
        · -- Show f n is continuous
          intro x
          rw [f_def]
          continuity
      rcases h10 with ⟨c, hc1, hc2, hc3⟩
      refine ⟨c, by linarith, by linarith, hc3⟩
    · -- n = 1
      have hn1 : n = 1 := by omega
      have h8 : f n 1 = 0 := by
        rw [show n = 1 by omega]
        rw [f_def]
        norm_num
      refine ⟨1, by norm_num, by norm_num, ?_⟩
      all_goals linarith
  have h9 : ∀ u v : ℝ, (0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0) → (0 ≤ v ∧ v ≤ 1 ∧ f n v = 0) → u = v := by
    intro u v ⟨hu0, hu1, hfu⟩ ⟨hv0, hv1, hfv⟩
    by_cases h : u < v
    · -- Assume u < v
      have h10 : f n u < f n v := by
        have h11 : u < v := by linarith
        have h12 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h13 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h14 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h15 : f n u < f n v := by
          rw [f_def, f_def]
          have h16 : ∀ x y : ℝ, x ≥ 0 → y ≥ 0 → x < y → (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) := by
            intro x y hx hy hxy
            have h17 : x < y := by linarith
            have h18 : ∀ k : ℕ, x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1) := by
              intro k
              have h19 : x ≤ y := by linarith
              have h20 : x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              assumption
            have h19 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) := by
              have h20 : x < y := h17
              have h21 : ∃ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1) < y ^ (k + 1) := by
                use 0
                constructor
                · -- Show 0 ∈ range n
                  simp
                  all_goals omega
                · -- Show x ^ 1 < y ^ 1
                  nlinarith
              have h22 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) := by
                apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
                · -- Show that for all k in range n, x^(k+1) ≤ y^(k+1)
                  intro k hk
                  specialize h18 k
                  all_goals try { tauto }
                · -- Show that exists k in range n such that x^(k+1) < y^(k+1)
                  rcases h21 with ⟨k, hk1, hk2⟩
                  refine ⟨k, by tauto, by nlinarith⟩
              linarith
            linarith
          specialize h16 u v (by linarith) (by linarith) (by linarith)
          nlinarith
        nlinarith
      linarith
    · -- Assume v ≤ u
      by_cases h' : v < u
      · -- Assume v < u
        have h10 : f n v < f n u := by
          have h11 : v < u := by linarith
          have h12 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h13 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h14 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h15 : f n v < f n u := by
            rw [f_def, f_def]
            have h16 : ∀ x y : ℝ, x ≥ 0 → y ≥ 0 → x < y → (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) := by
              intro x y hx hy hxy
              have h17 : x < y := by linarith
              have h18 : ∀ k : ℕ, x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1) := by
                intro k
                have h19 : x ≤ y := by linarith
                have h20 : x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1) := by
                  apply pow_le_pow_left
                  all_goals nlinarith
                assumption

theorem subq_I_1

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith

    rw [f_def]

      have h4 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ≥ 1 → (0 : ℝ) ^ k = 0 := by
        intro k hk
        have h5 : k > 0 := by omega

      have h6 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ∈ Finset.range n → k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
        intro k hk
        simp at hk ⊢
        all_goals omega
      have h7 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
        intro k hk
        have h8 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := h6 k hk
        have h9 : (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
          apply h4 (k + 1) h8
        simpa using h9
      linarith
    linarith
  have h3 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h5 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
        intro k
        simp
      rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => h5 k)]
      simp
    linarith
  have h4 : f n 0 < (0 : ℝ) := by
    rw [h2]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h5 : f n 1 ≥ (0 : ℝ) := by
    rw [h3]
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast (show n ≥ 1 by linarith)
    nlinarith
  have h6 : ∃ c : ℝ, c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n c = 0 := by
    have h7 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
      funext x
      rw [f_def]
      continuity
    have h8 : f n 0 < (0 : ℝ) := h4
    have h9 : f n 1 ≥ (0 : ℝ) := h5
    apply Continuous.exists_mem_set_of_le_of_lt_of_continuous
    · exact h7
    · norm_num
    · exact h8
    · exact h9
  rcases h6 with ⟨c, hc, hc_eq⟩
  use c
  constructor
  · -- Show that c satisfies the required conditions

    · -- Show f n c = 0

    have h7 : f n d = 0 := by

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)

  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith

        apply exists_mem_Icc_of_continuous_of_le_of_lt h15 (show (0 : ℝ) ≤ (1 : ℝ) by norm_num) (by linarith) (by linarith)

                    simpa using h26
                  linarith
            have h22 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
            linarith
          nlinarith
        · -- Assume u ≥ c
          by_cases h15 : u > c
          · -- Assume u > c
            have h16 : f n c < f n u := by
              have h19 : f n c = (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
                apply f_def
              have h20 : f n u = (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
                apply f_def
              rw [h19, h20]
              have h21 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
                · -- Show that for all k in range n, c^(k+1) ≤ u^(k+1)
                  intro k hk
                  have h22 : c ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                    have h23 : c ≤ u := by linarith
                    have h24 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
                      have : k ≥ 0 := by
                        omega
                      omega
                    have h25 : c ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                      apply pow_le_pow_left
                      all_goals nlinarith
                    linarith
                  linarith
                · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that c^(k+1) < u^(k+1)
                  use 0
                  constructor
                  · -- Show 0 in range n
                    simp at *
                    all_goals omega
                  · -- Show c^1 < u^1
                    have h26 : c < u := by linarith
                    have h27 : c ^ (0 + 1 : ℕ) < u ^ (0 + 1 : ℕ) := by
                      simpa using h26
                    linarith
              have h22 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, c ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
              linarith
            nlinarith
          · -- Assume u = c
            have h17 : u = c := by linarith
            linarith
      linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  by_cases h : n > 1
  · -- If n > 1
    have h3' : f n 1 > 0 := by
      rw [f_def]
      have h4 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
        have h5 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
          intro k
          have h6 : (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
            simp
          exact h6
        have h7 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
          calc
            (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1))
                = ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) := by
                  apply Finset.sum_congr
                  · rfl
                  · intro k hk
                    specialize h5 k
                    simpa using h5
            _ = (n : ℝ) := by simp
        exact h7
      rw [h4]
      have h9 : (n : ℝ) > (1 : ℝ) := by
        have hn2 : n > 1 := by omega
        have hn3 : n ≥ 2 := by omega
        have hn4 : (n : ℝ) ≥ (2 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast hn3
        nlinarith
      have h10 : (n : ℝ) - 1 > 0 := by nlinarith
      nlinarith
    have h2 : f n 0 = -1 := by
      rw [f_def]
      have h3 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
        have h4 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
          intro k
          have h5 : (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
            have h6 : k + 1 > 0 := by omega
            simp [pow_eq_zero_iff, h6]
          exact h5
        have h7 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
          apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
          intro k hk
          specialize h4 k
          simpa using h4
        exact h7
      rw [h3]
      all_goals norm_num
    have h5 : f n 0 < 0 := by
      rw [h2]
      all_goals norm_num
    have h7 : ∃ u : ℝ, u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) ∧ f n u = 0 := by
      apply ivt_of_continuous_of_le_of_lt
      · -- Show that f n is continuous
        fun_prop
      · -- Show that f n 0 ≤ 0
        rw [h2]
        all_goals norm_num
      · -- Show that 0 < f n 1
        linarith [h3']
    rcases h7 with ⟨u, hu, h8⟩
    use u
    constructor
    · -- Show 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0
      have h8 : u ≥ 0 := by
        have h9 : u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hu
        exact h9.1
      have h9 : u ≤ 1 := by
        have h10 : u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) := hu
        exact h10.2
      tauto
    · -- Show it's unique
      intro v h
      rcases h with ⟨hv0, hv1, h_eq⟩
      have h9 : f n v = 0 := h_eq
      have h10 : f n u = 0 := h8
      by_cases h14 : v < u
      · -- Assume v < u
        have h15 : f n v < f n u := by
          rw [f_def, f_def]
          have h16 : v < u := h14
          have h17 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h18 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h20 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) := by
            apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
            · -- Show that for all k in range n, v^(k+1) ≤ u^(k+1)
              intro k hk
              have h21 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                have h22 : v ≤ u := by linarith
                have h23 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h24 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h25 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                  apply pow_le_pow_left
                  all_goals nlinarith
                exact h25
              exact h21
            · -- Show there exists k in range n such that v^(k+1) < u^(k+1)
              use 0
              have h26 : 0 ∈ Finset.range n := by
                simp at *
                all_goals omega
              have h27 : v ^ (0 + 1) < u ^ (0 + 1) := by
                have h28 : v < u := h16
                have h29 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h30 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h31 : v ^ 1 < u ^ 1 := by
                  nlinarith
                simpa using h31
              all_goals try { tauto }
          have h28 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
          nlinarith
        linarith
      by_cases h15 : v > u
      · -- Assume v > u
        have h16 : f n v > f n u := by
          rw [f_def, f_def]
          have h17 : u < v := by linarith
          have h18 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h19 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h20 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) > (∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) := by
            apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
            · -- Show that for all k in range n, u^(k+1) ≤ v^(k+1)
              intro k hk
              have h21 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ v ^ (k + 1) := by
                have h22 : u ≤ v := by linarith
                have h23 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h24 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h25 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ v ^ (k + 1) := by
                  apply pow_le_pow_left
                  all_goals nlinarith
                exact h25
              exact h21
            · -- Show there exists k in range n such that u^(k+1) < v^(k+1)
              use 0
              have h26 : 0 ∈ Finset.range n := by
                simp at *
                all_goals omega
              have h27 : u ^ (0 + 1) < v ^ (0 + 1) := by
                have h28 : u < v := by linarith
                have h29 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h30 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h31 : u ^ 1 < v ^ 1 := by
                  nlinarith
                simpa using h31
              all_goals try { tauto }
          have h28 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 > (∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
          nlinarith
        linarith
      · -- v = u
        have h16 : v = u := by linarith
        linarith
  · -- Case n = 1
    have hn1 : n = 1 := by omega
    use 1
    constructor
    · -- Show 0 ≤ 1 ∧ 1 ≤ 1 ∧ f 1 1 = 0
      constructor
      · norm_num
      constructor
      · norm_num
      · -- Show f 1 1 = 0
        rw [f_def]
        rw [hn1]
        norm_num
    · -- Show it's unique
      intro u h
      rcases h with ⟨hu0, hu1, h_eq⟩
      rw [f_def] at h_eq
      rw [hn1] at h_eq
      norm_num at h_eq
      all_goals nlinarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h2 : f n 0 = -1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    simp
  have h3 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h5 : ∀ (n : ℕ), ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
        intro n
        induction n with
        | zero =>
          simp
        | succ n ih =>
          rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, ih]
          all_goals
            norm_num
      apply h5 n
    rw [h4]
  have h4 : f n 1 ≥ (0 : ℝ) := by
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast show n ≥ 1 by linarith
    nlinarith [h3]
  have h5 : f n 0 < (0 : ℝ) := by
    linarith [h2]
  use 1
  constructor
  · -- Show that 1 is a solution
    constructor
    · norm_num
    constructor
    · norm_num
    · -- Show that f n 1 = 0 when n = 1
      have h6 : n = 1 := by omega
      rw [h6] at h3
      norm_num at h3 ⊢
      all_goals linarith [h3]
  · -- Show that 1 is the unique solution
    intro c' hc0' hc1' hc2'
    have h7 : f n 1 = 0 := by
      have h6 : n = 1 := by omega
      rw [h6] at h3
      norm_num at h3 ⊢
      all_goals linarith [h3]
    -- Assume c' is a solution in [0,1], need to show c' = 1
    have hc2 : f n c' = 0 := hc2'
    by_cases h : c' < (1 : ℝ)
    · -- Assume c' < 1
      have h9 : f n c' < f n 1 := by
        have h10 : c' ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h11 : c' ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h12 : (1 : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h13 : (1 : ℝ) ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h14 : c' < (1 : ℝ) := by linarith
        have h15 : f n c' < f n 1 := by
          rw [f_def, f_def]
          have h17 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
            have h18 : c' < (1 : ℝ) := h14
            have h19 : 0 ∈ Finset.range n := by
              simp at *
              all_goals omega
            have h20 : (c' : ℝ) ^ (0 + 1) < (1 : ℝ) ^ (0 + 1) := by
              nlinarith
            have h21 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ∈ Finset.range n → (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
              intro k hk
              have h22 : k < n := by
                simp at hk ⊢
                all_goals omega
              have h23 : c' ≤ (1 : ℝ) := by linarith
              have h24 : (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals
                  nlinarith
              linarith
            apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
            · -- Show that for all k in range n, (c')^(k+1) ≤ 1^(k+1)
              intro k hk
              specialize h21 k hk
              linarith
            · -- Show that there exists at least one k in range n such that (c')^(k+1) < 1^(k+1)
              use 0
              constructor
              · -- Show 0 ∈ range n
                simp at *
                all_goals omega
              · -- Show (c')^1 < 1^1
                nlinarith
          have h17 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
          nlinarith
        linarith
      nlinarith [hc2, h7, h9]
    · -- Assume c' ≥ 1
      by_cases h : c' > (1 : ℝ)
      · -- Assume c' > 1
        have h9 : f n 1 < f n c' := by
          have h10 : (1 : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h11 : (1 : ℝ) ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h12 : c' ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h13 : c' ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h14 : (1 : ℝ) < c' := by linarith
          have h15 : f n 1 < f n c' := by
            rw [f_def, f_def]
            have h17 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
              have h18 : (1 : ℝ) < c' := h14
              have h19 : 0 ∈ Finset.range n := by
                simp at *
                all_goals omega
              have h20 : (1 : ℝ) ^ (0 + 1) < (c' : ℝ) ^ (0 + 1) := by
                nlinarith
              have h21 : ∀ k : ℕ, k ∈ Finset.range n → (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
                intro k hk
                have h22 : k < n := by
                  simp at hk ⊢
                  all_goals omega
                have h23 : (1 : ℝ) ≤ c' := by linarith
                have h24 : (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
                  apply pow_le_pow_left
                  all_goals
                    nlinarith
                linarith
              apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
              · -- Show that for all k in range n, 1^(k+1) ≤ (c')^(k+1)
                intro k hk
                specialize h21 k hk
                linarith
              · -- Show that there exists at least one k in range n such that 1^(k+1) < (c')^(k+1)
                use 0
                constructor
                · -- Show 0 ∈ range n
                  simp at *
                  all_goals omega
                · -- Show 1^1 < (c')^1
                  nlinarith
            have h17 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (c' : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
            nlinarith
          linarith
        nlinarith [hc2, h7, h9]
      · -- Assume c' = 1
        have h12 : c' = (1 : ℝ) := by linarith
        linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h2 : f n 0 ≤ 0 := by
    rw [f_def]
    norm_num
  have h3 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h4 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h5 : ∀ (n : ℕ), ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
        intro n
        induction n with
        | zero =>
          simp
        | succ n ih =>
          rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, ih]
          all_goals norm_num
      apply h5 n
    rw [h4]
    have h7 : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast show n ≥ (1 : ℕ) by linarith
    nlinarith
  have h12 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
    fun_prop
  have h13 : ∃ u : ℝ, u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) (1 : ℝ) ∧ f n u = 0 := by
    apply h12.exists_mem_Icc_of_left_le_of_right_ge
    all_goals try linarith
  rcases h13 with ⟨u, hu1, hu2⟩
  use u
  constructor
  · -- Show that u is indeed a solution in [0,1]
    exact ⟨hu1.1, hu1.2, hu2⟩
  · -- Show that the solution is unique
    intro v ⟨hv1, hv2, h_eq⟩
    by_cases h : v < u
    · -- Assume v < u
      have h16 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
      have h17 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
      have h18 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith [hu1.1]
      have h19 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith [hu1.2]
      have h20 : v < u := by linarith
      have h21 : f n v < f n u := by
        rw [f_def, f_def]
        have h22 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
          apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
          · -- Show that for all k in range n, v^(k+1) ≤ u^(k+1)
            intro k hk
            have h23 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
              have h24 : v ≤ u := by linarith
              have h25 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h26 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h27 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
                have h28 : k ≥ 0 := by
                  have h29 : k ∈ Finset.range n := by
                    exact hk
                  simp at h29 ⊢
                  all_goals omega
                omega
              have h28 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              exact h28
            linarith
          · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that v^(k+1) < u^(k+1)
            use 0
            constructor
            · -- Show 0 in range n
              simp at *
              all_goals omega
            · -- Show v^(0+1) < u^(0+1)
              have h23 : v ^ (0 + 1 : ℕ) < u ^ (0 + 1 : ℕ) := by
                have h24 : v < u := by linarith
                simp at *
                all_goals nlinarith
              simpa using h23
        have h23 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
        nlinarith
      have h22 : f n v < 0 := by linarith [hu2, h21]
      nlinarith [h_eq, h22]
    · -- If v >= u
      by_cases h' : v > u
      · -- Assume v > u
        have h16 : v > u := by linarith
        have h17 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith [hu1.1]
        have h18 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith [hu1.2]
        have h19 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
        have h20 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
        have h21 : f n u < f n v := by
          rw [f_def, f_def]
          have h22 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
            apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
            · -- Show that for all k in range n, u^(k+1) ≤ v^(k+1)
              intro k hk
              have h23 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ v ^ (k + 1) := by
                have h24 : u ≤ v := by linarith
                have h25 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h26 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith
                have h27 : k + 1 ≥ 1 := by
                  have h28 : k ≥ 0 := by
                    have h29 : k ∈ Finset.range n := by
                      exact hk
                    simp at h29 ⊢
                    all_goals omega
                  omega
                have h28 : u ^ (k + 1) ≤ v ^ (k + 1) := by
                  apply pow_le_pow_left
                  all_goals nlinarith
                exact h28
              linarith
            · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that u^(k+1) < v^(k+1)
              use 0
              constructor
              · -- Show 0 in range n
                simp at *
                all_goals omega
              · -- Show u^(0+1) < v^(0+1)
                have h23 : u ^ (0 + 1 : ℕ) < v ^ (0 + 1 : ℕ) := by
                  have h24 : u < v := by linarith
                  simp at *
                  all_goals nlinarith
                simpa using h23
          have h23 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (u : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (v : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
          nlinarith
        have h22 : f n u < 0 := by linarith [h_eq, h21]
        nlinarith [hu2, h22]
      · -- v ≤ u and not v > u, so v = u
        have h23 : v = u := by linarith
        linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h5 : f n (0 : ℝ) = -1 := by
    have h6 : f n (0 : ℝ) = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h6]
    have h7 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
      have h8 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (0 : ℝ) := by
        intro k
        induction k with
        | zero =>
          norm_num
        | succ k ih =>
          simp [pow_succ, mul_zero] at ih ⊢
      apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
      intro k hk
      specialize h8 k
      simpa using h8
    rw [h7]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h3 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    have h2 : f n 1 = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h2]
    have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
      simp
    rw [h4]
  have h10 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [h3]
    have h11 : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast show n ≥ 1 by linarith
    nlinarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h5 : f n (0 : ℝ) = -1 := by
    have h6 : f n (0 : ℝ) = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h6]
    have h7 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
      have h8 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        intro k
        ring_nf
      have h9 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) := by
        apply Finset.sum_congr
        · rfl
        · intro k hk
          specialize h8 k
          linarith
      rw [h9]
      simp
    rw [h7]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h3 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    have h2 : f n 1 = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h2]
    have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
      simp
    rw [h4]
  have h6 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [h3]
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast show (n : ℕ) ≥ (1 : ℕ) by omega
    nlinarith
  have h14 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
    have h15 : f n = fun x : ℝ => (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      funext x
      exact f_def n x
    rw [h15]
    apply Continuous.sub
    · apply continuous_finsetSum
      intro k hk
      apply continuous_pow
    · apply continuous_const
  -- Using the Intermediate Value Theorem.
  have h17 : ∃ c : ℝ, c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n c = 0 := by
    have h18 : f n (0 : ℝ) < 0 := by linarith
    have h19 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := h6
    have h21 : 0 ≤ (1 : ℝ) := by norm_num
    have h22 : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1 := by norm_num
    -- Since f n 0 < 0 and f n 1 ≥ 0, by continuity, there exists c in [0, 1] such that f n c = 0.
    have h23 : ∃ c : ℝ, c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n c = 0 := by
      apply Continuous.exists_mem_preimage_of_mem_image
      · exact h14
      · -- Show that 0 ∈ image(f n, [0, 1])
        have h24 : f n (0 : ℝ) < 0 := by linarith
        have h25 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := h6
        have h26 : 0 ∈ Set.Icc (f n (0 : ℝ)) (f n 1) := by
          constructor
          · -- Show that f n 0 ≤ 0
            linarith
          · -- Show that 0 ≤ f n 1
            linarith
        exact h26
      all_goals
        norm_num
    exact h23
  rcases h17 with ⟨c, hc1, hc2⟩
  have hc3 : 0 ≤ c := by
    have h1 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hc1
    exact h1.1
  have hc4 : c ≤ 1 := by
    have h1 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hc1
    exact h1.2
  use c
  constructor
  · -- Show that c is a solution
    exact ⟨hc3, hc4, hc2⟩
  · -- Show that c is unique
    intro d ⟨hd1, hd2, h_eq⟩
    have h14 : f n d = 0 := by linarith [h_eq]
    have h16 : f n c = 0 := hc2
    by_cases h21 : d < c
    · -- Assume d < c
      have h19 : f n d < f n c := by
        have h20 : f n d = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          apply f_def
        have h21 : f n c = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          apply f_def
        rw [h20, h21]
        have h22 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by

            intro k hk
            have h23 : (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
              have h24 : d ≤ c := by linarith
              have h25 : (d : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h26 : (c : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h27 : (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals linarith
              linarith
            linarith
          · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that d^(k+1) < c^(k+1)
            use 0
            constructor
            · -- Show that 0 is in range n
              simp at *
              all_goals omega
            · -- Show that d^1 < c^1
              have h28 : d < c := by linarith
              have h29 : (d : ℝ) ^ 1 < (c : ℝ) ^ 1 := by
                nlinarith
              simpa using h29
        have h23 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
        linarith
      linarith
    by_cases h22 : c < d
    · -- Assume c < d
      have h19 : f n c < f n d := by
        have h19 : f n c = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          apply f_def
        have h20 : f n d = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
          apply f_def
        rw [h19, h20]
        have h24 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) < ∑ k in Finset.range n, (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
          apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
          · -- Show that for all k in range n, c^(k+1) ≤ d^(k+1)
            intro k hk
            have h25 : (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
              have h26 : c ≤ d := by linarith
              have h27 : (c : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h28 : (d : ℝ) ≥ 0 := by linarith
              have h29 : (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) ≤ (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals linarith
              linarith
            linarith
          · -- Show that there exists k in range n such that c^(k+1) < d^(k+1)
            use 0
            constructor
            · -- Show that 0 is in range n
              simp at *
              all_goals omega
            · -- Show that c^1 < d^1
              have h28 : c < d := by linarith
              have h29 : (c : ℝ) ^ 1 < (d : ℝ) ^ 1 := by
                nlinarith
              simpa using h29
        have h25 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (c : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, (d : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
        linarith
      linarith
    · -- Case c = d
      have h23 : d = c := by linarith
      linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h3 : f n 0 = -1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
      apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
      intro k hk
      have h10 : k < n := by
        simp at hk ⊢
        all_goals omega
      have h11 : (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        have h12 : k + 1 > 0 := by
          omega
        simp [pow_pos, h12]
      simpa using h11
    simp [h4]
  have h4 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h5 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h6 : ∀ (n : ℕ), ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
        intro n
        induction n with
        | zero =>
          simp
        | succ n ih =>
          rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, ih]
          all_goals norm_num
      apply h6 n
    simp [h5]
  have h5 : f n 0 ≤ 0 := by
    rw [h3]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h6 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [h4]
    have hn1 : (n : ℝ) ≥ 1 := by exact_mod_cast show (n : ℕ) ≥ 1 by omega
    nlinarith
  have h7 : ∃ c : ℝ, c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n c = 0 := by
    have h13 : f n 0 ≤ 0 := h5
    have h14 : 0 ≤ f n 1 := by linarith [h6]
    apply Continuous.exists_eq_zero_of_le_of_le
    · -- Show that f n is continuous
      fun_prop
    all_goals try linarith
  rcases h7 with ⟨c, hc1, hc2⟩
  use c
  constructor
  · -- Show 0 ≤ c ∧ c ≤ 1 ∧ f n c = 0
    constructor
    · -- Show 0 ≤ c
      have h10 : c ≥ 0 := by
        have h11 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hc1
        exact h11.1
      linarith
    constructor
    · -- Show c ≤ 1
      have h11 : c ≤ 1 := by
        have h12 : c ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hc1

      linarith
    · -- Show f n c = 0

  · -- Show uniqueness

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith

    rw [f_def]

      have h7 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        intro k
        induction k with
        | zero => norm_num
        | succ k ih =>
          simp [pow_succ, mul_zero] at ih ⊢
      have h8 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = ∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) := by
        apply Finset.sum_congr
        · rfl
        · intro k hk
          specialize h7 k
          linarith
      rw [h8]
      simp
    linarith
  have h9 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    rw [f_def]
    have h10 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h11 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
        intro k
        induction k with
        | zero => norm_num
        | succ k ih =>
          simp [pow_succ] at ih ⊢
      have h12 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) := by
        apply Finset.sum_congr
        · rfl
        · intro k hk
          specialize h11 k
          linarith
      rw [h12]
      simp
    linarith
  have h19 : ∃ u : ℝ, u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
    have h20 : f n 0 < 0 := by linarith [h4]
    have h21 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by linarith [h9]
    have h22 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
      fun_prop
    rcases continuousOn_id (Set.mem_Icc.mpr ⟨by linarith, by linarith⟩) with ⟨x, hx, hfx⟩
    use x
    constructor
    · exact hx
    · -- Show f n x = 0
      simpa using hfx
  rcases h19 with ⟨u, hu1, hu2⟩
  use u
  constructor
  · -- Show that u is in the set

  · -- Show uniqueness
    intro v ⟨hv0, hv1, h11⟩
    by_cases h : v < u

          have h23 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
          have h24 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h25 : u ≤ 1 := by linarith
          have h26 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) := by
            apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
            · -- Show that for all k in range n, v^(k+1) ≤ u^(k+1)
              intro k hk
              have h28 : v ^ (k + 1) ≤ u ^ (k + 1) := by
                apply pow_le_pow_left
                all_goals nlinarith
              linarith
            · -- Show that there exists at least one k in range n such that v^(k+1) < u^(k+1)
              use 0
              simp at *
              all_goals nlinarith
          linarith
        have h27 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) - 1 < (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by linarith
        linarith
      have h20 : f n v < 0 := by

    · -- Assume v ≥ u
      by_cases h' : v > u
      · -- Assume v > u
        have h13 : f n v > f n u := by
          rw [f_def, f_def]
          have h20 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) > (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) := by
            have h21 : u < v := by linarith
            have h22 : u ≥ 0 := by linarith
            have h23 : v ≥ 0 := by linarith

            have h25 : v ≤ 1 := by linarith
            have h26 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, u ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, v ^ (k + 1)) := by

        have h20 : 0 < 0 := by

      have h21 : y ≤ 1 := by

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  have h3 : f n 0 = -1 := by
    have h4 : f n 0 = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h4]
    have h7 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = 0 := by
      have h8 : ∀ k : ℕ, (0 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 0 := by
        intro k
        simp
      simp_rw [h8]
      simp
    rw [h7]
    all_goals norm_num
  have h4 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := by
    have h5 : f n 1 = (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h5]
    have h6 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1)) = (n : ℝ) := by
      have h7 : ∀ k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = 1 := by
        intro k
        simp
      simp_rw [h7]
      simp
    rw [h6]
    have hn' : (n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast (show n ≥ 1 by linarith)
    nlinarith
  have h9 : Continuous (f n) := by
    funext x
    have h10 : f n x = (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
      apply f_def
    rw [h10]
    apply Continuous.sub
    · apply continuous_finset_sum
      intro k hk
      continuity
    · continuity
  have h12 : ∃ u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
    have h13 : f n 0 ≤ 0 := by linarith
    have h14 : f n 1 ≥ 0 := h4
    have h11 : Continuous (f n) := h9
    have h15 : ∃ u : ℝ, u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
      apply Continuous.exists_mem_preimage_of_le_of_le h11
      · -- Show that 0 ≤ 1
        norm_num
      · -- Show that f n 0 ≤ 0
        linarith
      · -- Show that 0 ≤ f n 1
        linarith
    rcases h15 with ⟨u, hu1, hu2⟩
    refine ⟨u, ?_, ?_⟩
    · -- Show that 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1
      exact hu1
    · -- Show that f n u = 0
      exact hu2
  rcases h12 with ⟨u, hu0, hu1, h13⟩
  have h9 : StrictMono (f n) := by
    intro x y hxy
    have h10 : f n x < f n y := by
      have h11 : f n x = (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
        apply f_def
      have h12 : f n y = (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) - 1 := by
        apply f_def
      rw [h11, h12]
      have h13 : (∑ k in Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) < (∑ k in Finset.range n, y ^ (k + 1)) := by
        apply Finset.sum_lt_sum
        · -- Show that for all k in range n, x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1)
          intro k hk
          have h14 : x ^ (k + 1) ≤ y ^ (k + 1) := by
            apply pow_le_pow_left
            all_goals nlinarith
          linarith
        · -- Show that there exists at least one k in range n such that x ^ (k + 1) < y ^ (k + 1)
          use 0
          have h14 : (0 : ℕ) ∈ Finset.range n := by
            simp at *
            all_goals omega
          have h15 : x ^ (0 + 1) < y ^ (0 + 1) := by
            simp
            all_goals nlinarith
          exact ⟨h14, by linarith⟩
      linarith
    linarith
  use u
  constructor
  · -- Show that u is indeed a solution
    tauto
  · -- Show that u is unique
    intro v hv
    rcases hv with ⟨hv0, hv1, h14⟩
    have h15 : f n u = 0 := by
      tauto
    have h16 : f n v = 0 := by
      tauto
    by_cases h : v < u
    · -- Assume v < u
      have h17 : f n v < f n u := by
        apply h9
        linarith
      linarith [h17, h15, h16]
    · -- Assume v ≥ u
      by_cases h' : v > u
      · -- Assume v > u
        have h18 : f n u < f n v := by
          apply h9
          linarith
        linarith [h18, h15, h16]
      · -- Assume v = u
        have h19 : v = u := by linarith
        linarith

theorem subq_I_1
    (f : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ)
    (f_def : ∀ n x, f n x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, x ^ (k + 1)) - 1)
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) :
    ∃! u : ℝ, 0 ≤ u ∧ u ≤ 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
  have h1 : n ≥ 1 := by linarith
  by_cases h : n > 1
  · -- Case n > 1
    have h6 : f n 1 > 0 := by
      have h3 : f n 1 = (n : ℝ) - 1 := by
        rw [f_def]
        have h4 : ∑ k in Finset.range n, (1 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) = (n : ℝ) := by
          simp
        rw [h4]
        all_goals norm_num
      rw [h3]
      have hn2 : n > 1 := by omega
      have hn3 : n ≥ 2 := by omega
      have hn4 : (n : ℝ) ≥ (2 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast hn3
      have hn5 : (n : ℝ) - 1 ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by linarith
      nlinarith
    have h7 : f n 0 < 0 := by
      have h4 : f n 0 = -1 := by
        rw [f_def]
        simp
      linarith
    have h9 : ∃ u : ℝ, u ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 ∧ f n u = 0 := by
      have h9 : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => f n x) := by
        funext x
        rw [f_def]
        apply continuous_finset_sum
        intro i hi
        apply continuous_pow
      have h10 : f n 0 < 0 := h7
      have h11 : f n 1 > 0 := h6
      apply Continuous.exists_mem_set_forall_mem_Icc_of_forall_mem_Icc_of_le_of_le h9
      · -- Show that f 0 ≤ 0
        linarith
      · -- Show that 0 ≤ f 1
        linarith
      all_goals
        norm_num
    rcases h9 with ⟨u, hu⟩
    rcases hu with ⟨hu1, hu2⟩
